epistemicModes.name: Empirical Measurement
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in observation, experiment, or measurement.

epistemicModes.name: Mathematical Derivation
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in formal calculation or derivation.

epistemicModes.name: Formal Logic
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in explicit logical structure or proof.

epistemicModes.name: Historical Attestation
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in dated records, witnesses, or historical transmission.

epistemicModes.name: Textual Philology
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in source texts, manuscripts, and language analysis.

epistemicModes.name: Traditional Rule
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in tradition-specific rules or inherited systems.

epistemicModes.name: Symbolic Interpretation
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in symbolic meaning within a defined tradition.

epistemicModes.name: Metaphysical Claim
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in metaphysical framing rather than empirical measurement.

epistemicModes.name: Theological Doctrine
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in doctrinal formulation and theological interpretation.

epistemicModes.name: Philosophical Argument
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in philosophical reasoning and argument structure.

epistemicModes.name: Comparative Analysis
epistemicModes.description: Claims grounded in comparison across sources, systems, or frameworks.

claimTypes.name: Computed Result
claimTypes.description: A result produced through deterministic calculation.

claimTypes.name: Formula Definition
claimTypes.description: A formal definition of a formula or rule.

claimTypes.name: Historical Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim about historical events, contexts, or attestations.

claimTypes.name: Textual Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim about wording, structure, or content of a text.

claimTypes.name: Translation Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim about translation choices or renderings.

claimTypes.name: Etymology Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim about word origins or lexical development.

claimTypes.name: Doctrinal Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim about doctrine, creed, or theology.

claimTypes.name: Symbolic Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim about symbolic correspondences or meanings.

claimTypes.name: Metaphysical Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim about being, causation, or metaphysical order.

claimTypes.name: Empirical Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim grounded in observation or experiment.

claimTypes.name: Interpretive Claim
claimTypes.description: A claim about interpretation of a source, symbol, or argument.

claimTypes.name: Logical Argument
claimTypes.description: A claim expressed as an explicit argument or proof.

sourceTypes.name: Critical Edition
sourceTypes.description: A critical edition of a source text.

sourceTypes.name: Manuscript Witness
sourceTypes.description: A manuscript witness or textual witness.

sourceTypes.name: Original Language Lexicon
sourceTypes.description: A lexicon for the original language of a source.

sourceTypes.name: Scholarly Translation
sourceTypes.description: A scholarly translation of a source.

sourceTypes.name: Textbook
sourceTypes.description: A textbook or formal instructional reference.

sourceTypes.name: Dataset
sourceTypes.description: A dataset used for empirical or computational analysis.

sourceTypes.name: Experimental Result
sourceTypes.description: An experimental result or measurement output.

sourceTypes.name: Modern Secondary Analysis
sourceTypes.description: A modern secondary analysis or review source.

validationRules.name: Scientific Empirical
validationRules.description: Validation profile for modern scientific and computational domains.
requirements: Empirical claims require empirical source grounding.
Computed results require deterministic tool or formula provenance.
prohibitions: Do not present symbolic interpretation as empirical measurement.

validationRules.name: Mathematical Formal
validationRules.description: Validation profile for formal mathematical content.
requirements: Formal claims require explicit definitions, derivations, or deterministic computation.
prohibitions: Do not infer empirical truth from formal validity alone.

validationRules.name: Symbolic Traditional
validationRules.description: Validation profile for symbolic and traditional systems.
requirements: Symbolic systems must preserve tradition-specific terminology.
Traditional rules must be separated from empirical causal claims.
prohibitions: Do not present traditional symbolic claims as validated physical causation.

validationRules.name: Theological Textual
validationRules.description: Validation profile for theology and related textual analysis.
requirements: Theological claims must distinguish primary text, doctrine, commentary, and later interpretation.
Translation claims require language or lexicon grounding.
prohibitions: Do not flatten doctrinal claims into empirical claims.

validationRules.name: Philological Textual
validationRules.description: Validation profile for philological and linguistic analysis.
requirements: Philological claims require source-text, language, or lexical grounding.
Etymology claims must distinguish attestation from speculation.
prohibitions: Do not treat translation choice as settled fact without source grounding.

validationRules.name: Philosophical Argument
validationRules.description: Validation profile for philosophical reasoning and interpretation.
requirements: Philosophical claims must distinguish argument structure from interpretation.
Metaphysical claims must be labeled as metaphysical, not empirical by default.
prohibitions: Do not present philosophical argument as experimental verification.

validationRules.name: Cosmology Scientific
validationRules.description: Validation profile for empirical and mathematical cosmology.
requirements: Scientific cosmology claims require empirical, mathematical, or dataset grounding.
prohibitions: Do not mix scientific cosmology with religious or metaphysical cosmology without explicit classification.

validationRules.name: Cosmology Traditional
validationRules.description: Validation profile for ancient, religious, or metaphysical cosmologies.
requirements: Traditional cosmologies must be classified by textual, symbolic, doctrinal, or metaphysical mode.
prohibitions: Do not present ancient, religious, or metaphysical cosmology as modern empirical cosmology.

domains.name: Mathematics
domains.description: Formal mathematical domain for deterministic derivation and proof.

domains.name: Physics
domains.description: Scientific domain for physical systems, laws, and measurable quantities.

domains.name: Physics General
domains.description: General physics classification for cross-cutting physical formulas and claims.

domains.name: Physics Mechanics
domains.description: Mechanics classification for motion, force, and related deterministic formulas.

domains.name: Physics Electromagnetism
domains.description: Electromagnetism classification for electric, magnetic, and circuit relations.

domains.name: Chemistry
domains.description: Scientific domain for chemical composition, reactions, and measurement.

domains.name: Neuroscience
domains.description: Scientific domain for nervous system structure, function, and data analysis.

domains.name: Astrology
domains.description: Traditional symbolic domain classified separately from empirical science.

domains.name: Cosmology
domains.description: Umbrella cosmology domain for traditional and scientific cosmological classifications.

domains.name: Cosmology Scientific
domains.description: Scientific cosmology classification for modern empirical and mathematical models.

domains.name: Cosmology Ancient
domains.description: Ancient cosmology classification for historically transmitted cosmological systems.

domains.name: Cosmology Religious
domains.description: Religious cosmology classification for doctrinal and text-grounded cosmological claims.

domains.name: Cosmology Metaphysical
domains.description: Metaphysical cosmology classification for non-empirical cosmological frameworks.

domains.name: Theology
domains.description: Theological domain for doctrine, scripture, commentary, and interpretation.

domains.name: Classics
domains.description: Philological domain for classical texts, manuscripts, and historical language study.

domains.name: Linguistics
domains.description: Philological domain for language structure, lexicon, and textual comparison.

domains.name: Philosophy
domains.description: Philosophical domain for arguments, interpretation, and metaphysical reasoning.
